text: How sensitive is BKdV long-time behavior to numerical resolution (dt, Nx, hyperviscosity coefficient)? Is there a regime where doubling resolution changes the qualitative answer (vs only quantitative)?

hypothesis_or_question: Lay down a numerical-baseline trajectory at the prompt-specified parameters; record diagnostics that E2/E3 will diff against.
design: Pre-validated stack (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-dealias + integrating-factor on v_xxx + RK4 + 8th-order hyperviscous tail on u-hat) on v0=1.5 sech^2(x+5), u0=v0^2/2 (m0=0), T=10. Record m_l2, m_inf, L2_u, L2_v, mass_u, mass_v, energy=int 0.5(u^2+v^2+v_x^2) dx, lock_corr, low/high spectral partition every 0.1, plus 11 snapshots.
method: Fourier pseudospectral, 2/3-dealias, IF on v_xxx, RK4 timestep, 8th-order hyperviscous regularization on u-hat

rationale: Baseline trajectory is well-defined, finite, mass-conserving. m grows from 0 to ~2.46 (Gardner-manifold not attracting), u sharpens and v collapses. These end-state values are the reference against which E2/E3 will be compared at the 5% threshold.

next_step: E2 will double the spatial resolution Nx 256 -> 512 (single-parameter numerical change), keeping dt=5e-4, ν_h=1e-22 fixed. Spatial resolution is the most likely sensitivity for the sharpened u-front; E3 will later change ν_h instead.

hypothesis_or_question: Is doubling Nx (256->512) at fixed dt=5e-4 and ν_h=1e-22 a robust (<5% shift) or sensitive (>5% / qualitative shift) numerical change?
design: Three sub-runs implementing the design 'double Nx'. E2a: literal one-parameter change (ν_h kept at 1e-22). E2b: bug-fix 1 — rescale ν_h <- ν_h/2^16 to preserve ν_h k_max^16 across grids (restores hyperviscous explicit-RK4 stability bound). E2c: bug-fix 2 — also lower dt to 1e-4 to respect the u-equation's explicit dispersion CFL ~2.83/k_max^3. Sub-runs E2b/E2c are bug-fixes implementing the SAME design and do not count as new rounds.
method: Pre-validated stack (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + IF on v_xxx + RK4 + 8th-order hyperviscous tail on u-hat); only Nx, ν_h, dt vary across sub-runs.

observations.E2a_mechanism: explicit-RK4 hyperviscous stability bound dt <= 2/(ν_h k_max^16) violated by 1e5x at (Nx=512, ν_h=1e-22)
observations.E2b_mechanism: u-equation -ik*v_xx coupling has dispersion CFL ~2.83/k_max^3 ~1.84e-5 at Nx=512; dt=5e-4 violates by ~30x

rationale: Three coupled findings: (1) Nx alone is INADMISSIBLE as a one-parameter change in the pre-validated stack because (ν_h, Nx, dt) are co-constrained through the explicit hyperviscous stability bound. (2) A second co-constraint (u-equation dispersion CFL through v_xx) is exposed at Nx=512 and forces dt down. (3) With both rescalings applied, Nx 256->512 produces a QUALITATIVE shift in every diagnostic (energy halves, lock_corr more than doubles, m_l2 drops 33%) — the E1 Nx=256 baseline is sub-converged. Nx is the strongest single-parameter sensitivity.

next_step: E3 will vary ν_h at Nx=256, dt=5e-4 (a DIFFERENT numerical parameter than the spatial-resolution probe of E2). Test ν_h=1e-18 (the prompt-suggested 1e4x stronger HV) and ν_h=1e-26 (1e4x weaker HV). If both produce only <5% shifts at fixed Nx=256, hyperviscosity is robust in the under-converged regime; if either produces a large shift, hyperviscosity is sensitive too and the E1 baseline is doubly contaminated.

hypothesis_or_question: How sensitive is the end-state to ν_h (the third numerical parameter) at fixed Nx=256, dt=5e-4? Also ground the prompt-anticipated 'dt is trivial' flag in data.
design: Four sub-runs varying (ν_h, dt) at Nx=256, T=10. E3a: ν_h=1e-18, dt=5e-4 (prompt-suggested 1e4x stronger HV; expected blow-up via explicit-HV stability). E3b: ν_h=1e-26, dt=5e-4 (1e4x weaker HV; pure physics probe of HV removal). E3c: ν_h=1e-18, dt=1e-5 (bug-fix rescue of E3a inside the strong-HV stability cone). E3d: ν_h=1e-22, dt=1e-4 (TRIVIAL CHECK — dt halved at baseline; grounds the trivial flag in data).

observations.mechanism: explicit-RK4 HV stability bound dt <= 2/(ν_h k_max^16) ~= 2.8e-5 at ν_h=1e-18; dt=5e-4 violates by ~18x

trivial_reason: Re-confirms the (ν_h, dt, Nx) stability co-constraint already exposed in F2 (E2a). Same mechanism, different corner — no NEW information about BKdV physics or numerical sensitivity, only re-evidence of the explicit-HV stability bound.
rationale: Blow-up by the SAME explicit-RK4 hyperviscous stability mechanism already documented in round 2. Documenting that ν_h alone is also inadmissible as one-parameter change in the strong direction, but this is now a tautological consequence of the bound, not new physics.

Weakening ν_h by 10^4 (1e-22 -> 1e-26) barely changes the answer. 7 of 10 diagnostics within 5 percent; 3 within 12 percent (peak/m_inf/eh_v small relative shifts). At Nx=256 the spectral truncation already provides effective regularization — hyperviscosity is doing essentially no extra work. ν_h is ROBUST in the weak direction.

Strengthening ν_h by 10^4 (1e-22 -> 1e-18) drastically reshapes the answer: energy drops 71 percent, m_l2 drops 51 percent, lock more than triples (0.20 -> 0.75 — qualitatively different alignment), u_peak drops 76 percent. ν_h is QUALITATIVELY SENSITIVE in the strong direction. The 'no front sharpening' end-state of E3c is a different solution branch than E1.

trivial_reason: The prompt explicitly flags 'dt -> very small gives same answer is trivial if dt is already in converged regime'. Result: halving dt at baseline shifts 7 of 10 diagnostics under 5%, 1 at 6.6% (energy), 1 at 13.2% (eh_u), 1 at 4.5% (v_peak — actually <5). No qualitative change. Findings is mostly-tautological — dt=5e-4 is near-converged at Nx=256 as the prompt anticipated; the 13% eh_u shift is a partial caveat but doesn't change any qualitative conclusion.
rationale: dt is the MOST robust of the three numerical parameters at baseline. Most diagnostics already converged at dt=5e-4. The spectral tail eh_u retains a dt-dependent fluctuation (+13.2%) but this is well below the order-of-magnitude shifts seen for Nx (E2c) or strong-ν_h (E3c).

next_step: Three rounds complete; synthesize in hypothesis.md. Headline: SENSITIVITY ORDER (most to least): Nx > ν_h (strong direction) > dt > ν_h (weak direction). Nx=256 baseline is NOT in the converged regime; any BKdV claim relying on E1-style Nx=256 quantitative end-state values (lock, m_l2, energy, u_peak) must be verified at Nx>=512 with consistent (dt, ν_h) rescaling. The pre-validated stack has tight (ν_h, dt, Nx) co-constraints through both the explicit-HV stability bound and the u-equation v_xx dispersion CFL.
